title: UnderControl Comment File
description: Schema for UnderControl comment files — YAML frontmatter + markdown body. Comments are attached to tasks via task_id and can anchor to specific text in the description or a note. This is the single source of truth for all clients.

task_id.description: UUID or prefix of the parent task. Required.

comment_id.description: Comment UUID. Present = update existing comment. Absent = create new comment.

parent_id.description: UUID or prefix of the parent comment. Present = this is a reply. Absent = top-level comment. Only two levels supported (top-level + replies).

anchor_target.description: Where the comment anchors to. Empty or absent = general (no anchor). 'description' = anchored to task description. 'note' = anchored to a specific note.

anchor_target_id.description: UUID of the note when anchor_target is 'note'. Not needed when anchor_target is 'description'.

quoted_text.description: The selected/highlighted text that this comment anchors to. Used for display and re-matching after content edits.

anchor_prefix.description: ~50 characters of text before the quoted selection. Used to disambiguate when the same text appears multiple times.

anchor_suffix.description: ~50 characters of text after the quoted selection. Used to disambiguate when the same text appears multiple times.

resolved.description: Whether the comment thread is resolved. Only meaningful for top-level comments. Resolving hides the highlight and collapses the thread.

body.description: Markdown content after the frontmatter closing '---'. Becomes the comment content.

file_format.description:
The complete file is a markdown file with YAML frontmatter:

---
task_id: {parent-task-uuid}
anchor_target: description
quoted_text: "the selected text"
---

Comment content in markdown...

create_vs_update.description:
Declarative semantics (kubectl-style):
- No 'comment_id' in frontmatter → CREATE new comment
- 'comment_id' present → UPDATE existing comment content

Routing:
- 'parent_id' present → create a REPLY to the specified comment
- 'anchor_target' present → create an ANCHORED comment
- Neither → create a GENERAL (non-anchored) comment

detection.description:
When processing a markdown file with 'ud apply -f', the presence of comment-specific fields distinguishes comments from notes:
- Has task_id + any of (comment_id, parent_id, anchor_target) → Comment
- Has task_id + note_id → Note
- Has task_id only → Note (backward compat)
- No task_id → Task

threading.description:
Comments support two-level threading:
- Top-level comments can be replies, anchored, or general
- Replies (parent_id set) are always children of a top-level comment
- Replies cannot have their own anchor or be replied to
- Resolving a top-level comment collapses the entire thread

anchoring.description:
Anchor matching strategy:
1. quoted_text is the primary match key
2. anchor_prefix + anchor_suffix provide disambiguation context
3. Match is attempted against the raw markdown of the target (description or note content)
4. If quoted_text is no longer found (content was edited), the anchor is shown as stale (strikethrough in UI)
5. Anchors do NOT modify the markdown content — they are stored separately in the comment entity

examples.description:
Create anchored comment:
---
task_id: abc123
anchor_target: description
quoted_text: "Remove old styled-components"
---
Should we keep this as a peer dependency?

Reply:
---
task_id: abc123
parent_id: def456
---
Good point, let's keep it optional.

General comment:
---
task_id: abc123
---
Can we deploy a Storybook preview?

Update:
---
task_id: abc123
comment_id: def456
---
Updated: also keep the theme provider.
